title.en: For crying out loud! Seriously! 아이참! 정말 왜 이래!

description.en: When someone is being frustrating or absurd! Express your feelings honestly with "Ai-cham" and "Jeong-mal wae i-rae".
description.ko: 상대방의 행동이 너무 답답하거나 어이가 없을 때! "아이참"과 "정말 왜 이래"로 내 마음을 솔직하게 표현해 봅니다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분~! 어느덧 10화네요! 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone~! It's already episode 10! I'm Mina.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. Look at this. I can type with my toes! Check it out.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존 씨..., 지금 대본 써야 하잖아요. 발로 타이핑을 하면 어떡해요~!
content.translation: John..., we have to write the script right now. What if you type with your toes~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wait. I almost got the letter A. Oh. Oops. I deleted everything.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "아이참~!"
content.translation: Oh boy~!

insight.title: 아이참
insight.description: An exclamation used when you are slightly annoyed or frustrated

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존~! "정말 왜 이래요~!"
content.translation: John! Seriously! Why are you being like this~!

insight.title: 정말 왜 이래
insight.usage_tip: Used when someone's behavior is repetitive and frustrating.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "For crying out loud!" I'm just trying to be creative. Mina.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 흐음- 창의적인 건 좋은데. 제발 일할 때는 집중해 주세요!
content.translation: Being creative is good. but please focus when we're working!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That was a perfect timing for "아이참!". It sounds so cute but I can feel the frustration.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! "아이참"은 화를 내기보다는. "아~ 정말 답답해" 라는 느낌이 강해요.
content.translation: 'Ai-cham' is more about feeling frustrated than being angry.

content.speaker: John
content.translation: Geez...,

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그리고 상대방이 왜 그러는지 도저히 이해가 안 될 때. "정말 왜 이래!" 라고 하면 딱이죠.
content.translation: And when you just can't understand why someone is doing that. 'Jeong-mal wae i-rae' is perfect.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "정말 왜 이래~!"
content.translation: Seriously. why are you being like this~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Can I use "Ai-cham" with a boss.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아니요~! 큰일 나요!
content.translation: No~! You'll be in big trouble!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이건 친한 친구나 동생한테만 쓰는 혼잣말 같은 거예요.
content.translation: This is like talking to yourself or saying it to close friends or younger people.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Imagine we are waiting for a bus. and it's been 30 minutes. but I keep saying. Oh. I think I see it! but it's just a truck.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 벌써 다섯 번째잖아요.
content.translation: John. This is the fifth time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: No. really! Look! That big red thing! Is that our bus.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "아이참~!" 존. 저건 소방차잖아요!
content.translation: Oh boy~! John. That's a fire truck!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh. My mistake. Maybe the next one.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "정말..., 왜 이래요~!" 존. 안경 안 썼어요?
content.translation: Seriously! Why are you being like this~! John. Are you not wearing your glasses?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Maybe I'm just too excited to go home.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아..., "정말 왜 이래요" 정말.
content.translation: Ah..., seriously. why are you like this. honestly.

content.speaker: John
content.text: These reactions are so essential for daily life.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 답답한 상황을 참지 말고 오늘 배운 표현을 써보세요!
content.translation: Right. Don't hold back in frustrating situations. Use the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "exasperated" reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number one. "아이참~!"
content.translation: Oh boy~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number two. "정말 왜 이래!"
content.translation: Seriously! Why are you being like this!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number three. "아니 진짜!"
content.translation: No. really!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number four. "도대체 왜 그래~!"
content.translation: Why on earth are you like this~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number five. "적당히 좀 해!"
content.translation: Give me a break! / That's enough!

insight.title: 적당히 좀 해
insight.usage_tip: Used when someone is overdoing something.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분 주변에는 답답하게 하는 사람이 없길 바랄게요!
content.translation: I hope there's no one around you who makes you frustrated!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's it for today. We'll be back with a more positive reaction next time.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 아이참. 또 실수했네. (Oh boy. I made a mistake again.)

vocabulary.meaning: Why are you being like this
vocabulary.example: 너 오늘 정말 왜 이래? (What's wrong with you today?)

vocabulary.meaning: To be frustrated. To be stuffy
vocabulary.example: 마음이 너무 답답해요. (I feel so frustrated.)
술은 적당히 마셔야 해요. (You should drink in moderation.)
도대체 그게 무슨 소리예요? (What on earth does that mean?)
공부할 때는 집중이 필요해요. (Focus is needed when studying.)
